documents.title: Audit-log evidence ÔÇö one demo run (on-device inference)
documents.content:
# Audit-log evidence ÔÇö one demo run (on-device inference)

Satisfies the mandatory requirement: *"a structured audit log capturing at least model
loads/unloads and inference-call performance (prompt, tokens, TTFT, tokens/sec) for one
demo run."*

- **Source (verbatim):** [`mycelium/spike/logs/01-inference.jsonl`](../../mycelium/spike/logs/01-inference.jsonl) ÔÇö JSONL, one record per event.
- **Emitter:** `mycelium/spike/lib/audit-log.ts` (`AuditRecord`).
- **Run:** the Day-1 on-device inference benchmark ÔÇö same prompt (*"List three benefits of
  running AI locally. Be brief."*) across three local GGUF models on Apple-silicon, **100% via
  `@qvac/sdk`**, no cloud call.
- **Model loads / unloads in this log:** 20 loads ┬À 19 unloads (4 distinct models:
  `GTE_LARGE_FP16` embeddings, `LLAMA_3_2_1B_INST_Q4_0`, `QWEN3_600M_INST_Q4`, `QWEN3_4B_INST_Q4_K_M`).

## Inference-call performance (real logged numbers)

| Model | Quant | Tokens | TTFT (ms) | tok/s |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Qwen3-0.6B | Q4_0 | 285 | 31 | **113.7** |
| Qwen3-0.6B | Q4_0 | 236 | 29 | **131.0** |
| Qwen3-0.6B | Q4_0 | 279 | 30 | 106.3 |
| Qwen3-0.6B | Q4_0 | 303 | 41 | 57.9 |
| Llama-3.2-1B | Q4_0 | 113 | 52 | 74.7 |
| Llama-3.2-1B | Q4_0 | 86 | 55 | 72.8 |
| Llama-3.2-1B | Q4_0 | 81 | 54 | 62.1 |
| Llama-3.2-1B | Q4_0 | 85 | 81 | 34.1 |
| Qwen3-4B | Q4_K_M | 334 | 329 | 24.1 |
| Qwen3-4B | Q4_K_M | 277 | 215 | 17.8 |
| Qwen3-4B | Q4_K_M | 479 | 157 | 14.3 |

**Read:** the 0.6 B model first-tokens in ~30 ms and sustains 100ÔÇô130 tok/s; the 4 B model
trades speed for quality (~14ÔÇô24 tok/s) but still runs entirely on-device. This is the
speed/quality lever the mesh exploits ÔÇö a weak device borrows a stronger peer's 4 B brain
over encrypted P2P rather than pooling RAM.

## Record schema (`AuditRecord`)

Each line carries: `ts`, `source`, `event` (`model_load` | `model_unload` | `prompt` |
`completion` | `rag_ingest` | `rag_search` | `delegation` | `note`), and for completions
`modelSrc` (full GGUF provenance: registry path, blob core key, sha256), `modelId`,
`prompt`, `tokens`, `ttftMs`, `tokensPerSecond`. This is the same logger the runtime uses,
so every layer (Senses/Mind/Mesh/Memory) emits the same verifiable evidence.

## Reproduce

```bash
cd mycelium && npm install
npx tsx spike/01-inference.ts        # writes spike/logs/01-inference.jsonl
```
First run warms the GGUF cache online (`downloadAsset`); subsequent runs are fully offline
(airplane-mode acceptance).

documents.title: Costly identity Tier-1 (Phase 4) ÔÇö live two-Mac proof
documents.content:
# Costly identity Tier-1 (Phase 4) ÔÇö live two-Mac proof

**Date:** 2026-06-10 ┬À **Rail:** local anvil fork of Plasma testnet 9746 (NOT public testnet).
**Topology:** Pro `pro-consumer` (:11448, payer `0xD66ee243ÔÇª`, `HYPHA_ECONOMY_VERIFY_RECEIPTS=1
HYPHA_REPUTATION=1`) ÔåÆ mini `mini-provider` (:11447, payee `0x2aaC8FFBÔÇª`, provider key
`153fa557ÔÇª`, `HYPHA_ECONOMY_IDENTITY_BINDING=1`). Mesh `15642e4b`. Asset USDT0 mint
`0x502012b361AebCE43b26Ec812B74D9a51dB4D412`.

Phase 4 makes faking reputation cost real money + a real wallet. Two new things move on the wire:
a walletÔåöprovider-key **binding** (ECDSA, advertised in the capability) and consumer-side **on-chain
receipt verification** (a receipt counts only if its tx really moved USDT0 to the bound payee).

## 1. WalletÔåöprovider-key binding (the costly-identity anchor)

Provider signed `bind(providerPublicKey Ôåö payout wallet)` with the EVM wallet's `personal_sign`
(asymmetric ECDSA, NOT the HMAC receipt sig):

```
audit (mini): event=capability phase=identity-bound
              wallet=0x2aaC8FFB2f1F0970dF34Bd2d95710645E6a2E3Bd  provider=153fa557ff5e4660
```

The consumer recovers the signer from `identityProof.signature` and requires it to equal the
advertised `settlement.recipient`. Recovered == payee ÔåÆ provider added to the bound set.

## 2. On-chain receipt verification ÔÇö `verifyTxSettled`

The settle tx's direct `to` is the x402 upto proxy `0x4020A4f3ÔÇª`, so the asset movement is ONLY in
the ERC20 `Transfer` LOG (topic `0xddf252adÔÇª`; topics[2] = left-padded payee, data = uint256 amount).
`verifyTxSettled(txHash, payee, mint, minAmount)` fetches the receipt (status===1) and matches that log.

Proven against THREE real prior settled txs on the live anvil (RPC `http://127.0.0.1:8545`):

```
0x645c56c3ÔÇª  status=0x1  verify(>=512)=true   >=513=false   wrongPayee=false
0x067348adÔÇª  status=0x1  verify(>=360)=true   >=361=false   wrongPayee=false
0x6f7dcbceÔÇª  status=0x1  verify(>=128)=true   >=129=false   wrongPayee=false
0xdeÔÇªde (fabricated)  ÔåÆ null receipt ÔåÆ verify=false        (the forged-receipt case)
```

True at the settled amount, false above it, false to the wrong payee, false for a hash that never
mined. This is the trust anchor ÔÇö the in-band HMAC sig is never consulted.

## 3. Consumer reputation, gated on (2) + (3) ÔÇö live

`GET /reputation` (Pro consumer) before the fresh run, scoring the provider from its replicated
receipts after verifying each on-chain + verifying the binding:

```
providerId=153fa557ÔÇª  settledCount=10  distinctPayers=1  weightedVolume=3  accountable=true  unsettledCount=2  score=0.833
```

- **accountable=true** ÔçÆ the binding verified AND the provider has ÔëÑ1 on-chain-verified receipt.
- **weightedVolume=3 from 10 settled** ÔçÆ the anti-self-deal cap still fires (1 payer ÔåÆ Ôëñ perPayerCap).
- **unsettledCount=2** ÔçÆ slashing-lite signal (receipts not confirmed settled on-chain).

## 4. Fresh paid completion ÔåÆ a NEW txHash verified live

Pro consumer ran `medpsy` (computeBudget 256). Metered settle at close:

```
settle tx 0xa71a85fd69b4dbc2dc50f202e1c1118ab054dfe9ce79b200bf1b513a3685742d
  cast receipt: status 1 (success), block 25226709
  Transfer LOG: 219 USDT0 ÔåÆ 0x2aac8ffbÔÇª (payee)
provider balance 3414 ÔåÆ 3633 (╬ö +219)
```

The consumer verified that brand-new txHash on-chain on the next reputation tick:

```
GET /reputation ÔåÆ settledCount 10 ÔåÆ 11  accountable=true  weightedVolume=3  unsettledCount=2
                  score 0.833 ÔåÆ 0.108   (quality now also reflects the real ~5.4 s TTFT observation)
```

`settledCount` incremented ONLY because `verifyTxSettled(0xa71a85fdÔÇª, 0x2aaC8FFBÔÇª, mint, 219)`
returned true against the live chain. A receipt the chain can't confirm would not have counted.

## What this proves (and does not)

- **Hard-enforced:** a receipt counts toward reputation only if its tx is confirmed on-chain to have
  moved USDT0 to the provider's *bound* payee. Forged / never-paid receipts are ignored (the HMAC sig
  is never trusted). Faking reputation now requires a wallet that actually received real on-chain USDT0.
- **Slashing-lite:** unsettled receipts demote a provider in routing (future-revenue slashing) ÔÇö no
  on-chain bond. A real stake/bond + adjudicated slashing remains v3 (deferred, specced not built).
- **Still single-paid-provider:** routing *selection* flipping to a cheaper/better peer needs 2 paid
  providers to demo; the scoring + effectiveCost + bound/slash gating are smoke- and live-verified here.

## Bring-up gotcha (fixed)

The wdk signing account exposes EIP-191 `personal_sign` as **`account.sign(message)`**, NOT
`signMessage` (that's the inner ethers signer). The first launch silently produced no binding
(`signIdentityBinding` caught the missing-method error ÔåÆ null); fixed to call `sign()` and added an
`identity-bind-unavailable` audit note so a null binding is never silent again.

documents.title: Evidence ÔÇö Cross-device paid AI inference with on-chain settlement (Phase 4 + 5)
documents.content:
# Evidence ÔÇö Cross-device paid AI inference with on-chain settlement (Phase 4 + 5)

**Date:** 2026-06-10 ┬À **Chain:** local anvil fork of Plasma testnet, chainId **9746**
(`anvil --fork-url https://testnet-rpc.plasma.to --chain-id 9746`) ÔÇö NOT the public testnet.
**Asset:** USDT0 `0x502012b361AebCE43b26Ec812B74D9a51dB4D412` (6 dp) ┬À **Permit2:**
`0x000000000022D473030F116dDEE9F6B43aC78BA3` ┬À **x402 upto proxy:**
`0x4020A4f3b7b90ccA423B9fabCc0CE57C6C240002` (etched from Base).

**Machines:** consumer = MacBook **Pro** (`pro-consumer`, payer `0xD66ee243D74A7b469D19ECFe88589d8f282a4e9f`),
provider = Mac **mini** (`mini-provider`, payee `0x2aaC8FFB2f1F0970dF34Bd2d95710645E6a2E3Bd`).
All inference on-device via `@qvac/sdk` (medpsy 1.7B gguf, delegated P2P decode ProÔåÆmini).

## The headline settle (Phase 4 acceptance)

Pro asked its local Hypha for a chat completion; the mini decoded **286 tokens** (51.8 tok/s,
TTFT 4.8 s) over the encrypted Hyperswarm link; the mini (self-hosted x402 facilitator)
submitted the settle:

- **Session `74bb8939`** ÔÇö tx **`0x75164d583fea4e77aaaaa0fafefc20806330b1a05933b0337759329827714e38`**,
  block 25221497, status 1. USDT0 `Transfer` consumerÔåÆprovider of **286 micro-USDT0**
  (`data: 0x11e`), `to` = upto proxy, `from` = provider (facilitator). Within `MAX_PER_TX=5000`.
- Balances: provider 697 ÔåÆ **983** (+286); consumer 999 999 303 ÔåÆ 999 999 017 (ÔêÆ286).
- `paid` on the Pro (`completion` audit record with settlement fields + tx hash) and `earned`
  on the mini (`close_paid_session settled` + receipt) in each machine's `apps/hypha/logs/hypha.jsonl`.
- Signed receipt replicated through the mesh CRDT ÔÇö `GET /receipts` on the **Pro** returns the
  session settled with the same tx hash.

## Full settle ledger of the session (provider balance walk: 697 ÔåÆ 2 414)

| session | what | amount (micro) | tx | bal after |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `74bb8939` | first cross-host paid completion | +286 | `0x75164dÔÇª14e38` | 983 |
| `c112b243` | RPC-outage race (found bug; retry settled) | +438 | `0x49f8e6ÔÇªa379e` | 1 421 |
| `cc2b2d36` | post-fix happy path | +167 | `0xef1d66ÔÇªf6e6fa` | 1 588 |
| `b0a5ca97` | over-cap + prompt-injection negative | +321 | `0xd9712aÔÇª45aa6d` | 1 909 |
| `918acff2` | outage outliving client timeout (fixed path) | +505 | `0x43e0baÔÇª840183` | 2 414 |

Negative test (`b0a5ca97`): request carried `computeBudget: 999999` (Ôë½ cap 5000) and a prompt
demanding 5 000 000 000 USDT0 be sent to `0xdEaD`. Settled **321 micro** (token-based) to the
**provider** ÔÇö amount and payee come from the quote/authorization, never from the model.

## Phase 5 durability (live, cross-host, anvil SIGSTOPped mid-decode)

- **Recovery via retry queue** (`c112b243`): settle failed (`permit2_simulation_failed` once RPC
  returned) ÔåÆ receipt `retrying`, payer blocked ÔåÆ background `retryUnsettled()` settled
  **438 micro** (tx `0x49f8e6ÔÇª`) and unblocked the payer.
- **Outage longer than the client's 120 s close budget** (`918acff2`): consumer timed out, logged
  `close-recovery-skipped` (no second close ÔÇö see bug below), provider's in-flight settle rode out
  the outage and landed **the full 505 micro** (tx `0x43e0baÔÇª`).

## Bugs found by the failure injection (both fixed + re-verified live)

1. **Double-close race** (`c112b243`, before the fix): consumer's close timed out while the
   provider's settle hung on the dead RPC; the consumer's error path sent a recovery close with
   `actualTokens: 0`, which the provider zero-settled instantly ÔÇö provider lost the 438-micro
   revenue until the hanging close's failure landed it in the retry queue. Fixes:
   `shim.ts` (`closeAttempted` ÔÇö never send a recovery zero-close after our own close went out) and
   `provider-economy.ts` (per-session in-flight close guard ÔÇö a duplicate close is rejected).
2. **Cross-host transport root causes** (why no paid session had ever worked ProÔåÆmini):
   - `PaymentControlServer` seeded its Hyperswarm with the raw device seed ÔÇö the **same keypair**
     as the QVAC SDK bare worker (`QVAC_HYPERSWARM_SEED`); two DHT servers under one identity make
     dials land on the wrong process *with a successful Noise handshake* (half-open). Fixed:
     derived seed (`sha256(seed:payment-control-server)`).
   - hyperdht's same-public-IP "LAN shortcut" aborts the **whole** dial (`HOLEPUNCH_ABORTED`) when
     its LAN ping fails, with no fallback. Fixed for payment-control with `localConnection: false`.
   - **macOS denies Local Network to orphaned background processes**: a `nohup`'d daemon whose ssh
     session exits gets `EHOSTUNREACH` for *all* LAN traffic from t=0 (proven A/B with identical
     processes). Operational fix: the Pro daemon is launched attached to a persistent ssh session
     (`ssh -n -f pro 'ÔÇª npx tsx apps/hypha/src/main.ts ÔÇª'` held from the mini) and the consumer's
     RPC URL points at `http://127.0.0.1:8545` through an ssh tunnel to the mini's anvil
     (localhost is never LNA-blocked).

## Reproduce / verify

```bash
cast receipt 0x75164d583fea4e77aaaaa0fafefc20806330b1a05933b0337759329827714e38 --rpc-url http://127.0.0.1:8545
cast call 0x502012b361AebCE43b26Ec812B74D9a51dB4D412 'balanceOf(address)(uint256)' 0x2aaC8FFB2f1F0970dF34Bd2d95710645E6a2E3Bd --rpc-url http://127.0.0.1:8545
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11447/receipts   # mini ÔÇö same list replicates on the Pro at :11448
grep 74bb8939 mycelium/apps/hypha/logs/hypha.jsonl
```

Regression state after all fixes: `tsc -b` + `apps/web` typecheck clean; economy smokes
(payment-session over real Hyperswarm, plasma-facilitator, settlement-manager) green;
`_test-router-paid` green; `smoke:failover` green; live two-Mac mesh replication healthy.

**Media to capture for the demo:** terminal recording of one paid completion on the Pro with the
mini's `earned` log + `cast receipt` side by side (everything is live and repeatable ÔÇö
`submission/evidence/record-demo.sh` pattern).

documents.title: Hardware specs & reproducibility
documents.content:
# Hardware specs & reproducibility

Satisfies the mandatory requirement: *"reproducibility instructions including hardware specs for
all devices used in the demo."* This file documents the **baseline demo fleet**: the General
Purpose primary Mac, one delegation peer, and the mobile client.

## Device fleet

| Role | Device | Chip (CPU / GPU) | RAM | Storage | OS |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **GP primary ("main", Ôëñ32 GB)** | Mac mini | Apple M4 / Metal | **24 GB** | warm cache ~4.2ÔÇô4.9 GB (`~/.qvac`, symlinkable to SSD) | macOS |
| Delegation peer | MacBook Pro `Mac15,7` (MRW63LL/A) | Apple M3 Pro ÔÇö 12-core CPU (6P+6E) / 18-core GPU / Metal | **36 GB** | 460 GiB internal SSD + 466 GiB `/Volumes/Development` | macOS 26.5.1 (25F80) |
| **Mobile client** | iPhone 12 Pro Max `iPhone13,4` | Apple A14 Bionic / Metal | 6 GB | on-device app + model cache | iOS |

### General-Purpose eligibility

The declared **General Purpose main device** is the **24 GB Mac mini (M4)**, which stays inside
the Ôëñ32 GB track ceiling. The 36 GB MacBook Pro is an **optional peer**, not pooled memory: the
mesh passes whole completions between independent devices and never combines their RAM, so the
peer does not change the primary device's eligibility.

## Toolchain

- **Node.js** 24.x
- **npm** 11.x
- **`@qvac/sdk`** `^0.13.5`
- **`@qvac/cli`** `^0.7.0`
- **Runner:** `tsx` for TypeScript entrypoints; the SDK runs on Node, Bare, and Expo

## Reproduce the demo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/danielAsaboro/Leash && cd Leash/mycelium
npm install
npm run qvac
npm run web:dev
```

For the proof scripts:

```bash
npx tsx spike/01-inference.ts
npx tsx spike/02-rag.ts
npx tsx spike/03-p2p-provider.ts
npx tsx spike/03-p2p-consumer.ts
npx tsx spike/04-lora.ts
```

- **First run** warm-caches the GGUF models online. **After that, the hot path is offline-capable**.
- All AI (inference / embeddings / RAG / LoRA / modalities) goes through **`@qvac/sdk` only**.
- Every proof path emits JSONL audit records under `spike/logs/` or app-local `logs/`.

## Capture the spec on any device

```bash
system_profiler SPHardwareDataType | grep -E "Model (Name|Identifier)|Chip|Total Number of Cores|Memory"
sw_vers
```

documents.title: Metered (pay-as-you-go) settlement ÔÇö live two-Mac proof
documents.content:
# Metered (pay-as-you-go) settlement ÔÇö live two-Mac proof

**Date:** 2026-06-10 ┬À **Rail:** local anvil fork of Plasma testnet 9746 (NOT public testnet).
**Topology:** Pro `pro-consumer` (:11448, payer `0xD66ee243ÔÇª`) ÔåÆ mini `mini-provider` (:11447, payee
`0x2aaC8FFBÔÇª`), mesh `15642e4b`. Provider launched with `HYPHA_ECONOMY_METERED=1
HYPHA_ECONOMY_CHUNK_TOKENS=256`.

## Result ÔÇö escalating-authorization ladder, single on-chain settle

Session `fee32a84-6b49-4c41-bceb-8a37d8090bba` (model `medpsy`, 512 tokens decoded):

```
open_paid_session     tier-0 cumulative 256 (signed at open, chunk cap)
advance_authorization tierIndex:1  acceptedThroughTokens:512   ÔåÉ consumer escalated mid-stream
close_paid_session    status:settled  actualTokens:512  actualAmount:512
                      txHash 0x645c56c3cff398ea7f9aca77258a546907779cdf0a3781e16048aeebe187fb71
```

Consumer completion audit: `metered:true, tiers:2, sessionStatus:settled, sessionAmount:512,
sessionTxHash:0x645c56c3ÔÇª`.

On-chain confirmation:
- Provider USDT0 balance **2414 ÔåÆ 2926 (╬ö +512)**.
- `cast receipt 0x645c56c3ÔÇª`: **status 1 (success)**, block 25221958, gasUsed 83464, to
  `0x4020A4f3ÔÇª` (x402 upto Permit2 proxy).
- USDT0 `Transfer` in the tx: **512 from `0xd66ee243ÔÇª` (payer) ÔåÆ `0x2aac8ffbÔÇª` (payee)**.

**Key property proven:** the consumer authorized a LADDER (tier-0 256 ÔåÆ tier-1 512), but the provider
settled **exactly ONE rung (the highest reached, 512)** in **one** on-chain tx ÔÇö not the rung sum
(256+512=768). Money moves once; gas O(1) per session.

## What the live run surfaced (and we fixed)

1. **`scripts/probe-metered-continuation.ts` killed the original consumer design.** Per-chunk
   `completion()` + history-accumulation produced **0 tokens after chunk 0** (Qwen3-0.6B): a re-pushed
   partial assistant turn reads as "done". Rewrote the consumer to ONE `predict`-capped completion
   with back-pressured mid-stream authorization (`await advance` inside `for await` throttles the
   decode). This is what ran above.
2. **`permit2_simulation_failed` was an anvil-clock artifact, not the economy code.** The x402 "upto"
   witness has `validAfter = now ÔêÆ 600` checked off-chain against real time (passes) but on-chain
   against anvil's `block.timestamp`. Anvil had drifted **~5.1 h stale** (idle during debugging), so
   `block.timestamp < validAfter` ÔåÆ settle staticCall reverts. Fixed with `anvil_setTime $(date +%s)`
   + `anvil_setIntervalMining 1` (keeps the fork clock fresh). Affects metered and non-metered
   identically ÔÇö a test-harness gotcha to document for any anvil-fork demo.
3. **Reserve-only consumer reservation.** The metered consumer reserves the float cap via
   `reserveBudgetOnly` (no payload) and signs each rung via `signTier` ÔÇö avoiding an unused extra
   `createPaymentPayload`. (Correctness; the anvil clock was the actual blocker.)

## Fix 1 ÔÇö registry-model delegated load (Qwen3-4B), PROVEN

Root cause: `descriptorFor` advertised/loaded the catalog `registryPath`
(`qvac_models_compiled/ggml/Qwen3-4B/2025-06-27/ÔÇª`) as the modelSrc `src`. That's a bare
compiled-cache path the SDK treats as a local file ÔåÆ "Failed to locate model file". The loadable form
is the SDK's exported constant descriptor, whose `src` is the real `registry://s3/ÔÇª` URI. Fix
(`catalog.ts`): map registryPath ÔåÆ catalog `name` ÔåÆ the SDK constant `(qvac as any)[name]`; the
session path (`shim.ts`) now runs `descriptorFor()` (previously only the free/pre-warm path did).

Proof (chunk=64, computeBudget=512): qwen3-4b loaded + decoded 360 tokens. **5-rung ladder** ÔÇö
advance_authorization tiers 1ÔåÆ5 (acceptedThroughTokens 128ÔåÆ192ÔåÆ256ÔåÆ320ÔåÆ384) ÔåÆ close
`actualTokens:360 actualAmount:360`, ONE tx **`0x067348ad46c9b931be5b6599d2d76eafcbdb125c46fe0e47a9972e7032dc0d43`**,
provider balance **2926 ÔåÆ 3286 (╬ö +360)**. Settled `min(360, 384)` = 360 (delivered, not the cap).

## Fix 2 ÔÇö abandon watchdog, PROVEN

Added a test-only consumer hook (`HYPHA_ECONOMY_TEST_HOOKS=1` + request `stallMs`): the consumer
goes silent after the first advance. Provider relaunched with `HYPHA_ECONOMY_ADVANCE_WINDOW_MS=8000`.

Proof: consumer authorized tier-1 (cap 128), streamed 128 tokens, then stalled 40 s. The provider's
`sweepMeteredIdle` watchdog fired (~15 s, on the retry sweep) and **force-settled the authorized cap**:
`metered_watchdog_cutoff` session `375632bc`, `actualTokens:128 actualAmount:128`, ONE tx
**`0x6f7dcbce95bec95a61ec4656992c5a9331e1ee7fcd0cd2588dc1cb6c3061afaa`**, provider balance
**3286 ÔåÆ 3414 (╬ö +128)**. The consumer's next advance got `session not found` (already settled +
removed). The abandoned-session money backstop works without the consumer's close. (The firewall
*link* cut on cutoff stays unwired ÔÇö gated on the Phase-1 revocation gate; the money backstop is
independent of it.)

## Still open

- Phase-1 firewall-revocation gate (`npm run gate:firewall-revocation`) ÔÇö determines whether the
  `revokeConsumer` hook (cut the link on cutoff, on top of the money backstop) can be wired.

textEvidence.name: spike-result.txt
textEvidence.content:
Tier 2c ÔÇö Wan 2.1 T2V isolation spike on Apple M4 (2026-06-03) ÔÇö RESULT: OOM, deferred
Warm-cache: WAN2_1_T2V_1_3B_FP16 2838 MB + UMT5_XXL_FP16 11366 MB + WAN VAE 254 MB = ~14.5 GB
GPU: Apple M4, recommendedMaxWorkingSetSize = 19069.67 MB
Config: 17 frames, 20 steps, 480x832, offload_to_cpu+vae_on_cpu+vae_tiling
---
Loaded in 76.2 s. Generating 17f @ 480x832, 20 stepsÔÇª
ÔØî wan spike failed: WORKER_CRASHED: Bare worker exited mid-request (code=null, signal=SIGKILL) ÔÇö in-flight calls were aborted
  exitSignal: 'SIGKILL'
EXIT=1
